laws.title: Public Law 17-124 | Law Enforcement Accountability Act (LEAA)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. A free-standing, generally-applicable regulatory statute (use-of-force, canine, and de-escalation rules for law enforcement). No self-repeal or sunset; SEC2 anti-supersession clause. It amends no numbered USAR Code section by express strike/insert.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'Law Enforcement Accountability Act' / 'LEAA'.
provisions.evidence: "This bill shall be referred to as the 'Law Enforcement Accountability Act' or 'LEAA'."

provisions.text_summary: Severability plus clause that Act may not be superseded unless later law expressly so declares.
provisions.evidence: "This Act shall not be superseded by any newer law unless that law specifically declares its intent to supersede..."

provisions.text_summary: Effective upon passage and signature.
provisions.evidence: "This bill shall be effective upon passage and signature of the President."

provisions.text_summary: Definitions (apprehension, bite, canine handler, use of force, deadly force, resistance tiers, etc.).
provisions.evidence: "SEC4.DEFINITIONS For the purpose of this bill-"
provisions.notes: General permanent law; candidate for a new section in a law-enforcement title (no existing target given).

provisions.text_summary: Rules for tactical/non-tactical canine deployment, call-off, handler duties, medical treatment.
provisions.evidence: "Canine deployments can be authorised for the following assignments..."
provisions.notes: Two SEC5 headings appear (drafting duplication: Canines and Use of Force).

provisions.text_summary: Objective-reasonableness use-of-force framework, de-escalation, deadly-force limits, medical aid, duty to intervene/report.
provisions.evidence: "law enforcement shall only use force that is objectively reasonable."
provisions.notes: Duplicate SEC5 numbering in source.

provisions.text_summary: Attorney General and agency leadership to enact enforcement policies.
provisions.evidence: "The Attorney General of the United States, along with other Department and Agency Leadership shall enact policies and procedures to enforce the provisions of this act."
provisions.notes: No SEC6 in source (gap).

current_implementation.detail: Draft added only a boilerplate note at t18/s1 and declared 'Current'. Because this is substantive permanent general law, it should be enacted as real Code section(s), not merely a note. No quoted-content dump present; Trello link present.

recommended_actions: Codify as a new permanent chapter/sections (use-of-force + canine standards) in the appropriate law-enforcement placement rather than a bare note.
Remove Trello link from rp-pl017124-codification.
Correct duplicated SEC5 numbering and the missing SEC6 in editorial notes.

laws.title: Public Law 17-125 | We Don't Read Minds Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Free-standing directive requiring each executive department's Inspector General to submit monthly status reports to Congress; penalty for noncompliance. No self-repeal or sunset. Listed under 'Repealed Laws' but body shows no repeal of itself.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'We Don't Read Minds Act'.
provisions.evidence: "may also be referred to as the 'We Don't Read Minds Act'"

provisions.text_summary: Purpose: mandate monthly Cabinet updates to Congress.
provisions.evidence: "to mandate that the Cabinet of the United States deliver monthly updates..."

provisions.text_summary: Each executive department's Inspector General must submit monthly reports to Congress.
provisions.evidence: "the Inspector General from each executive department must be tasked with delivering and submitting monthly reports to the Congress..."
provisions.notes: Reporting duty; candidate for a permanent section (likely Title 5).

provisions.text_summary: House may open inquiry/investigation and fine a non-reporting OIG up to $10,000, plus further punishment.
provisions.evidence: "the OIG being fined of up to $10,000 in USD and any further punishment deemed necessary by the House."

provisions.text_summary: Effective immediately after passage and Presidential signature.
provisions.evidence: "This bill will take effect immediately after passage through both houses of Congress and a signature from the President."
provisions.notes: Section numbered 6 though prior is 3 (drafting gap).

current_implementation.detail: Draft added a note at t5/s101 and declared 'Repealed or superseded' with no supporting evidence in the source (source shows no self-repeal). Substantive reporting mandate warrants an actual Code section. Trello link present.

recommended_actions: Codify the IG monthly-reporting mandate as a permanent Title 5 section rather than a note; verify against later batch laws whether any repeal exists before marking repealed.
Remove 'Repealed or superseded' assertion unless a repealing later law is identified.
Remove Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 17-126 | Special Events Act
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Establishes special-event permitting authority in the Department of Commerce and Labor for the District of Columbia. Local/District-focused regulatory scheme; no self-repeal or sunset.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'Special Events Act' / 'SEA'.
provisions.evidence: "referred to as the 'Special Events Act' or 'SEA'."

provisions.text_summary: Severability + anti-supersession.
provisions.evidence: "This Act shall be severable..."

provisions.text_summary: Effective upon passage and signature.
provisions.evidence: "effective upon passage and signature of the President."

provisions.text_summary: Definitions of permit, special event, forgery.
provisions.evidence: "'Special Event' means any sports events, pageants, public spectator attractions..."
provisions.notes: Numbered SEC5 (no SEC4); three separate SEC5 blocks in source (Definitions, Establishing Permits, Enforcement).

provisions.text_summary: DOCAL power to issue DC special-event permits (max 2 hours), permit contents, database, transmission to police, denial grounds, application requirements.
provisions.evidence: "issue permits for special events within the district of columbia, for a time not exceeding 2 hours."
provisions.notes: Purely District-of-Columbia administrative permitting; ordinarily not permanent U.S. Code.

provisions.text_summary: Forgery of a permit is a misdemeanour (fine up to $15,000 [text says 'fifty thousand'] and 4-day imprisonment); revocation/blacklist; enforcement authority.
provisions.evidence: "Any forgery of a Special Events Permit shall be classed as a misdemeanour..."
provisions.notes: Internal inconsistency: '$15,000' written after 'fifty thousand dollars'.

current_implementation.detail: Draft placed a boilerplate note at t50/s1 (Title 50 War & National Defense) which is a poor subject-matter anchor for a DC special-events permitting scheme; declared 'Repealed or superseded' without source support. Trello link present. Note: PL-019-144 (PAMA) later references PL-17-126 as still operative, undercutting a repeal claim.

recommended_actions: Exclude from permanent Code as local/District regulatory law; keep a concise honest historical note (not at Title 50).
Do not assert repeal absent a repealing instrument.
Remove Trello link; fix mis-anchored Title 50 placement.

laws.title: Public Law 17-130 | Oath and Offices Protection (OOP)
laws.status_basis: Full source present. Free-standing statute requiring officials to take the 5 U.S. Code 3331 oath within set timeframes, with forfeiture for failure; references 3331 as an existing anchor but does not restate/replace its text.

provisions.text_summary: Short title 'OOP'.

provisions.evidence: "The term 'oath of office' refers to the oath established in 5U.S.Code3331."
provisions.notes: Cross-reference to 3331, not an amendment of it.

provisions.text_summary: Oath must be taken within 5 days (elected), 48 hours (appointed), 24 hours (acting); failure forfeits office; excludes President/VP.
provisions.evidence: "Failure to be sworn in within the allotted timeframe shall result in said person forfeiting their position or office."
provisions.notes: Candidate for a new Title 5 section adjacent to 3331; NOT a textual amendment of 3331.

Remove Trello link; correct mis-anchored placement.

provisions.evidence: "Section 3 of the Employment Rights and Limitations Act, Subsection B I, Public Law 15-100 shall be henceforth read;"
provisions.notes: Target is a prior public law; identify wherever PL 15-100 is codified (likely Title 29). If PL 15-100 is only a statutory note, amend that note text.

provisions.text_summary: Amends PL 15-100 Section 5, Subsection F: no person may hold three+ concurrent agencies/positions unless constitutionally/statutorily authorized; plus limitation on holding a PAS office and an elected office simultaneously (except US Attorney/Assistant US Attorney).

provisions.text_summary: Severability + anti-supersession.
provisions.evidence: "This Act shall be severable..."

provisions.text_summary: Effective immediately after passage/signature.
provisions.evidence: "This bill will take effect immediately after passage..."

The Act makes real amendments to PL 15-100's provisions, which must be effected in whatever text carries PL 15-100.

Remove the quoted-content dump and Trello link.
Drop unsupported 'repealed' status.
